article_meta.title: Police turned him over to ICE

reasoning: The summary is largely neutral and avoids the article’s emotive framing (e.g., replaces “hail of bullets” with clinical phrasing). It includes one mildly subjective adjective (“controversial” 287(g)) and states “targeted ICE agents” without attribution, but does not otherwise adopt loaded language. It omits some perspectives present in the article and lists only “USA TODAY” as a source.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; one descriptor (“controversial”) edges toward evaluative but not pervasive.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Asserts the shooter targeted ICE agents without attribution; otherwise facts track the article.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids emotive terms like “hail of bullets”; uses clinical phrasing.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No ad hominem or personal attacks.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives beyond the un-attributed targeting statement.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes victim background and tragedy; omits officials’ defense of 287(g) and claims agents tried to help.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out pro-287(g) viewpoints and federal officials’ accounts from the summary and key facts.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Generally neutral; “undocumented immigrant” is standard newsroom style.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone matches inherently tragic events; no amplification.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege one source type; mainly narrative facts.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source’s emotive framing; minor lapse with “controversial.”
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Key perspectives (ICE/DHS statements, law enforcement support for 287(g)) are missing; sources_represented lists only the outlet.
